detail.flocksHelpTitle: Open AI Edit in the right panel
detail.noMdDescHint: Generate a human-readable workflow edit document
detail.mdUpdatedHint: AI has updated the flow description. Please review the edit document and provide feedback in the right chat.
detail.generateEditDocTitle: Generate a readable edit document from the flow, nodes, and workflow.md
detail.generateWorkflowTitle: Ask the right AI editor to regenerate workflow files from the current edit document and existing workflow files
detail.regenerateWorkflowConfirmDesc: Workflow files have changed. Regenerate the workflow?
detail.editDocEmptyHint: Generate a human-readable plain-text document from the current flow diagram, node descriptions, and workflow.md.

rightPanel.deleteConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to delete workflow "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

nodeInfo.nodeNotExecutedYet: This node has not executed in the latest run
nodeInfo.runNodeUnsupported: This node type is not supported yet
nodeInfo.runNodeUnsupportedDesc: Branch and Loop nodes are not supported for isolated execution yet.
nodeInfo.runNodeInputObjectRequired: Execution inputs must be a JSON object

run.testSummaryIdle: Inputs ready for a test run
run.rootObjectRequired: Input parameters must be a JSON object
run.jsonFormatError: JSON format error, please check input
run.cancelRequested: Cancellation requested. Waiting for the current step to stop.
run.publishDesc: Publish the workflow as a standalone API service with a dedicated invoke URL and API Key.
run.dockerStarting: Starting Docker container, may take 1-2 minutes...
run.driverLocalDesc: Recommended first; starts faster for local development and debugging
run.driverDockerDesc: Runs isolated, best for validating container deployment
run.kafkaInputsJsonError: Kafka Inputs must be a valid JSON object
run.kafkaHint: When enabled, Flocks consumes messages from the given broker/topic and passes them to workflow inputs (default key: kafka_message).
run.kafkaInputsHint: These extra inputs are merged with the consumed Kafka message; the actual kafka_message/kafka_value/kafka_record payload always wins on key collisions.
run.pollerInputsJsonError: Inputs must be a valid JSON object
run.pollerInputsHint: By default this prefills from the current workflow's Sample Inputs when available. You can also enter any inputs JSON that matches this workflow.
run.pollerHint: This is a generic background polling capability for any workflow. The poller only schedules repeated runs; idempotency, state caches, and input semantics remain defined by the workflow itself.
run.syslogHint: When enabled, Flocks listens for syslog on the given address/port and passes parsed payloads to workflow inputs (default key: syslog_message).
run.syslogPortError: Invalid port: must be an integer between 1 and 65535

chat.inputPlaceholder: Describe your workflow changes... (Enter to send)

welcome.title: Workflow "{{name}}"
welcome.descPart1: Describe your changes directly, or start with Flocks Help to clarify the setup. When a change is needed, AI will use the edit document first. Review the left
welcome.descPart2: view to confirm the flow is correct before syncing the JSON definition file.
welcome.guideDesc: Click once and I will load the configuration skill, read the workflow, then lead you through the single most useful next step.
welcome.guidePrimaryDesc: Let Flocks help configure this workflow
welcome.guideSampleDesc: Validate inputs and outputs with a sample
welcome.guideNote: By default this only guides you. Any file change will show a diff first.
welcome.guidePrompt: Please start the smart configuration guide for this workflow. The workflow ID is {{id}}, the workflow directory is {{dir}}, the MD file is {{dir}}workflow.md, and the JSON file is {{dir}}workflow.json. First call skill_load(name="{{configSkillName}}") and strictly follow that skill. Ask only one question at a time, put the recommended default first, and do not modify any file before showing a diff and getting my confirmation.
welcome.auditPrompt: Please inspect the current workflow configuration and docs. The workflow ID is {{id}}, the workflow directory is {{dir}}, the MD file is {{dir}}workflow.md, and the JSON file is {{dir}}workflow.json. Tell me what is already configured, what is missing, and the single most recommended next step. Do not modify files.
welcome.samplePrompt: I want to validate this workflow with one realistic sample. The workflow ID is {{id}}, the workflow directory is {{dir}}, the MD file is {{dir}}workflow.md, and the JSON file is {{dir}}workflow.json. Tell me what sample format I should paste, and after I paste it, check field mapping, normalization output, and likely deduplication behavior. Do not modify files.
welcome.bullet1: Modify node logic or execution code
welcome.bullet2: Adjust branch conditions and data flow
welcome.tipPart1: Before making changes, check the current flow description in the left
welcome.tipPart2: tab. Clear editing intent leads to better results.

create.canvasHint: Describe your requirements in the right chat box, AI will automatically generate a workflow and visualize it here

overview.chatHint: Describe your requirements in the "Chat" tab, workflow details will be filled automatically after generation

chat.inputPlaceholder: Describe the workflow you want to create... (Enter to send)
chat.contextMessage: The user wants to create a Flocks workflow, please use the workflow-builder skill to complete it.
chat.welcomeMessage:
Hello! I'm your Workflow Creation Assistant 🚀

Please describe the workflow you'd like to create, for example:

- **Threat Intelligence**: IP / Domain / File Hash lookups
- **Data Processing**: Multi-source collection, cleaning, correlation
- **Automated Response**: Security alert handling, incident response

The more detail you provide, the more accurate the generated workflow will be. You can also click the example questions below to get started quickly!
exampleQuestions: Create an IP threat intelligence query workflow that takes an IP address, queries multiple intelligence sources, and generates a summary report
Create a domain analysis workflow for WHOIS lookup, DNS resolution, and historical record cross-analysis
Design a file hash detection workflow that cross-validates hash values across multiple threat intelligence platforms
Build a phishing site detection workflow that analyzes URL features, page content, and SSL certificate information
Create a security incident response workflow that automatically collects, correlates, and analyzes security alerts to generate remediation recommendations

editor.promptPlaceholder: Supports Jinja2 templates, e.g. Analyze: {{ content }}
editor.propertiesHint: After modifying, click "Apply Changes" to save, or click the top "Save" button to save the entire workflow.
